description: CPIC gene-drug pairs with clinical actionability. Maps pharmacogenes to affected medications with dosing guideline levels.

CYP2D6.note: Major metabolizer of 25% of all drugs. Poor metabolizers at risk of toxicity or therapeutic failure depending on whether drug is pro-drug.

CYP2C19.note: Critical for clopidogrel activation (pro-drug). Poor metabolizers at high risk of stent thrombosis.

CYP2C9.note: Primary warfarin metabolizer. Combined with VKORC1 for warfarin dosing.

CYP3A5.note: CYP3A5 expressers require higher tacrolimus doses to achieve therapeutic levels.

CYP2B6.note: Poor metabolizers at risk of efavirenz CNS toxicity.

CYP1A2.note: Highly inducible by smoking. Relevant for clozapine dosing.

CYP4F2.note: Affects vitamin K metabolism; modifies warfarin dose requirement.

DPYD.note: LIFE-THREATENING: Poor metabolizers at risk of fatal fluoropyrimidine toxicity. Pre-treatment testing mandatory in many guidelines.

TPMT.note: LIFE-THREATENING: Poor metabolizers at risk of fatal myelosuppression. Dose reduction or alternative required.

NUDT15.note: Particularly important in East Asian populations. Compounds with TPMT status.

UGT1A1.note: UGT1A1*28 (Gilbert syndrome allele) increases irinotecan toxicity risk.

VKORC1.note: Combined with CYP2C9 for warfarin dosing algorithm. VKORC1 -1639G>A major dose determinant.

NAT2.note: Slow acetylators at increased risk of drug-induced lupus (hydralazine) and hepatotoxicity (isoniazid).

SLCO1B1.note: Decreased function variants increase statin myopathy risk. Simvastatin >20mg contraindicated in SLCO1B1 poor function.

ABCG2.note: Poor function increases rosuvastatin exposure; dose cap recommended.

G6PD.note: Deficient individuals at risk of hemolytic anemia with oxidative drugs. Screen before prescribing.

HLA-A.note: HLA-A*31:01 carriers at increased risk of carbamazepine hypersensitivity (DRESS/SJS).

HLA-B.note: HLA-B*57:01: abacavir hypersensitivity (MANDATORY pre-test). HLA-B*15:02: carbamazepine SJS/TEN (Southeast Asian). HLA-B*58:01: allopurinol SJS/TEN.

RYR1.note: Malignant hyperthermia susceptibility. Volatile anesthetics and depolarizing muscle relaxants contraindicated.

CACNA1S.note: Malignant hyperthermia susceptibility (less common than RYR1).

MT-RNR1.note: m.1555A>G carriers at risk of irreversible aminoglycoside-induced hearing loss.

CFTR.note: CFTR genotype determines ivacaftor eligibility for cystic fibrosis treatment.

IFNL3.note: IL28B genotype predicts hepatitis C treatment response.
